title: Christella grandis
taxonomy: Christella grandis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or the Thelypteridaceae family. This family is part of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns. The classification of Christella grandis also places it within the order Thelypterales. It is further classified within the genus Christella, a genus of ferns that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. Christella grandis is native to Australia, where it is often found in coastal and mountainous regions.

It has been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as skin problems such as eczema and dermatitis.
